title: BR-001: Backlog re-entry — evidence-backed remediation queue (docs-only)
intent: Reconstruct a current, evidence-backed, dependency-aware production remediation queue from the existing backlog (handoffs, Dev Orders, audits, branches, PRs, TODO/FIXME, tests) at the origin/main d716d16 baseline. Establish the remediation program under docs/remediation/: a charter (tiered A/B/C adjudication, 13 dispositions, living-ledger burn-down), a source inventory, a per-item adjudication ledger (34 items BR-001..BR-034), unfinished-sprint / defect / deferred registers, a dependency map, an advisory priority model, an execution queue in evidence-derived waves, a Wave 0 verification record (full services/api pytest vs d716d16: 21 failed / 8155 passed on a local Python 3.14 toolchain, recorded as directional not authoritative), and a single bounded next-candidate packet (saw_lab/rmos store-layer keyword-argument wiring). Determine what production work remains valid and in what order; do NOT remediate any defect, expand governance, or continue WF-A01 / Investigation 024. Documentation plus this governance manifest only; no production code, schema, route, or build change.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Adds docs/remediation/ (the BR-001 remediation program) and a one-line link from docs/index.md, plus this CBSP21 manifest. No source, schema, route, or build file is touched. The remediation program is documentation: a charter, source inventory, adjudication ledger (34 items), unfinished-sprint/defect/deferred registers, dependency map, priority model, execution queue, Wave 0 verification record, and next-candidate packet.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: No prior docs/remediation/ program existed; the repo's backlog was scattered across ~166 handoffs, 40 audits, 157 branches, and PR history with no current, evidence-backed, single ordering. This reconstructs and orders that backlog without duplicating it (concise evidence references, not copied reports) and without fixing anything.

commands_run: git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD -> only docs/ plus .cbsp21/patches/br-001-backlog-reentry.json (production-neutral)
docs/remediation link + identifier + disposition-vocabulary + queue<->ledger validation -> pass (see docs/remediation/README.md closeout)

cd services/api && python -m pytest --no-cov --continue-on-collection-errors -> 21 failed / 8155 passed / 19 xfailed / 1 xpassed on local Python 3.14 (directional; CI 3.11 stack is authoritative) -> docs/remediation/WAVE_0_VERIFICATION.md

files.intent: This CBSP21 patch manifest declaring the docs-only BR-001 change set.
Add a Maintenance link to the remediation program.
Program charter: tiers, dispositions, boundaries, living-ledger design.
Every backlog source examined with review status.
Per-item adjudication ledger (34 items, one disposition each).
Authorized-but-incomplete work, kept distinct from debt.
Parked Lab/research/external work excluded from the queue.
The one bounded next remediation target packet.
Wave 0 pytest baseline with the toolchain caveat.
Local remediation index + closeout validation record.

no_backend_no_schema: No backend API, route, schema, persistence, or build change. All changes are documentation under docs/ plus this CBSP21 governance manifest under .cbsp21/patches/. No production code is touched.
